message:
fix(connector_inbound_bridge): drive dedup clock from local time, not attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms (ft-dijpe)

## Defect

route_signal at line 495 used `signal.timestamp_ms` directly as the
dedup "now" argument. That field is documented at line 132 as
"Timestamp of the signal at the SOURCE" — it arrives from the remote
connector over the wire. A malicious or buggy upstream that claims
monotonically-increasing timestamps on replayed signals bypasses the
dedup window:

1. Route signal X, cid='spam', timestamp_ms=0 → inserted_at=0.
2. Route signal X again, cid='spam', timestamp_ms=ttl+1 →
   evict_expired sees now=ttl+1, front-entry inserted=0,
   ttl+1 - 0 > ttl → pops 'spam'. Cache now empty. check finds no
   'spam' entry → inserts + returns `true` (new). Replay succeeds.

Repeat indefinitely by monotonically increasing the claimed
timestamp. Every duplicate signal is re-routed to the pattern engine
+ EventBus, re-firing every downstream side effect (workflow
triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Fix

Drive `SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record`'s "now_ms" from the
local SystemTime::now() instead of the wire value. The
signal.timestamp_ms field stays as analytics/telemetry data (e.g.,
source→sink latency measurement), but it can no longer manipulate
the dedup eviction window.

SystemTime + UNIX_EPOCH were already in scope at line 17, so the fix
is a 4-line change at the dedup call site.

## Test

`bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp`:

 * Bridge configured with dedup_ttl_secs=60 (huge real-time window).
 * Route signal A with cid='replay-target' and timestamp_ms=0 →
   asserted not deduplicated.
 * Route signal A again with cid='replay-target' and
   timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 (far future) → asserted deduplicated.
 * Pre-fix: the second call returned deduplicated=false because the
   attacker's timestamp evicted the live cache entry. Post-fix: the
   local clock put both calls in the same real-time window, the
   replay is caught, signals_deduplicated=1 in telemetry.

## Verification

CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p1-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core \
  --lib --no-default-features --features asupersync-runtime \
  connector_inbound_bridge::
→ 44 passed, 0 failed (43 existing + 1 new regression test).

## Scope note

Outbound bridge is unaffected — it uses its own local `now_ms` for
internal state. This defect only shapes the inbound surface, which
is by design the agent-owned ingress for webhook/API signals where
the remote side is semi-trusted at best.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
